content:
The user wants me to extract the content of the image into a JSON format.

**Image Analysis:**
1.  **Header:**
    *   Part numbers: TPS562200, TPS563200
    *   Document code: SLVSCB0E
    *   Date: JANUARY 2014 – REVISED MAY 2023
    *   Logo: TEXAS INSTRUMENTS
    *   Website: www.ti.com

2.  **Section Title:**
    *   6.7 Typical Characteristics TPS562200

3.  **General Note:**
    *   $V_{IN}$ = 12 V (unless otherwise noted).

4.  **Graph 1 (Top Left):**
    *   Y-axis: $I_{CC}$ - Supply Current (µA)
    *   X-axis: $T_J$ - Junction Temperature (°C)
    *   Caption: Figure 6-1. Supply Current vs Junction Temperature

5.  **Graph 2 (Top Right):**
    *   Y-axis: $I_{VCCSDN}$ - Shutdown Current (µA)
    *   X-axis: $T_J$ - Junction Temperature (°C)
    *   Note: EN = 0 V
    *   Caption: Figure 6-2. VIN Shutdown Current vs Junction Temperature

6.  **Graph 3 (Middle Left):**
    *   Y-axis: VFB Voltage (V)
    *   X-axis: $T_J$ - Junction Temperature (°C)
    *   Note: $I_O$ = 1 A
    *   Caption: Figure 6-3. Vfb Voltage vs Junction Temperature

7.  **Graph 4 (Middle Right):**
    *   Y-axis: EN Input Current (µA)
    *   X-axis: EN Input Voltage (V)
    *   Caption: Figure 6-4. En Current vs En Voltage

8.  **Graph 5 (Bottom Left):**
    *   Y-axis: Efficiency (%)
    *   X-axis: $I_{OUT}$ - Output Current (A)
    *   Curves for $V_{OUT}$ = 5 V, $V_{OUT}$ = 3.3 V, $V_{OUT}$ = 1.8 V
    *   Caption: Figure 6-5. Efficiency vs Output Current

9.  **Graph 6 (Bottom Right):**
    *   Y-axis: Efficiency (%)
    *   X-axis: $I_{OUT}$ - Output Current (A)
    *   Curves for $V_{OUT}$ = 3.3 V, $V_{OUT}$ = 1.8 V
    *   Note: $V_{IN}$ = 5 V
    *   Caption: Figure 6-6. Efficiency vs Output Current

10. **Footer:**
    *   Page number: 8
    *   Link: Submit Document Feedback
    *   Copyright: Copyright © 2023 Texas Instruments Incorporated
    *   Product Folder Links: TPS562200 TPS563200

**Constructing the JSON:**
I will create a list of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents a distinct element (header, title, graph, caption, footer).

*   Header elements
*   Title "6.7 Typical Characteristics TPS562200"
*   Note "$V_{IN}$ = 12 V (unless otherwise noted)."
*   Graph 1 data and caption
*   Graph 2 data and caption
*   Graph 3 data and caption
*   Graph 4 data and caption
*   Graph 5 data and caption
*   Graph 6 data and caption
*   Footer elements

Let's refine the graph data extraction. I won't extract every single data point but will describe the axes and the general trend or specific labels visible.

*   **Graph 1:**
    *   Y: $I_{CC}$ - Supply Current (µA) from 0 to 400
    *   X: $T_J$ - Junction Temperature (°C) from -50 to 150
    *   Trend: Slight increase from ~190 to ~230 µA.
    *   Caption: Figure 6-1. Supply Current vs Junction Temperature

*   **Graph 2:**
    *   Y: $I_{VCCSDN}$ - Shutdown Current (µA) from 0 to 6
    *   X: $T_J$ - Junction Temperature (°C) from -50 to 150
    *   Trend: Increase from ~2.5 to ~5.5 µA.
    *   Note: EN = 0 V
    *   Caption: Figure 6-2. VIN Shutdown Current vs Junction Temperature

*   **Graph 3:**
    *   Y: VFB Voltage (V) from 0.750 to 0.780
    *   X: $T_J$ - Junction Temperature (°C) from -50 to 150
    *   Trend: Slight decrease from ~0.775 to ~0.765 V.
    *   Note: $I_O$ = 1 A
    *   Caption: Figure 6-3. Vfb Voltage vs Junction Temperature

*   **Graph 4:**
    *   Y: EN Input Current (µA) from -10 to 60
    *   X: EN Input Voltage (V) from 0 to 18
    *   Trend: Linear increase from ~-5 to ~55 µA.
    *   Caption: Figure 6-4. En Current vs En Voltage

*   **Graph 5:**
    *   Y: Efficiency (%) from 30 to 100
    *   X: $I_{OUT}$ - Output Current (A) from 0.001 to 10
    *   Curves: Three curves for $V_{OUT}$ = 5 V, 3.3 V, 1.8 V. All show efficiency increasing with current then plateauing or slightly dropping.
    *   Caption: Figure 6-5. Efficiency vs Output Current

*   **Graph 6:**
    *   Y: Efficiency (%) from 0 to 100
    *   X: $I_{OUT}$ - Output Current (A) from 0.001 to 10
    *   Curves: Two curves for $V_{OUT}$ = 3.3 V, 1.8 V. Similar shape to Graph 5.
    *   Note: $V_{IN}$ = 5 V
    *   Caption: Figure 6-6. Efficiency vs Output Current

I will format this into the final JSON output.
```json
[{"bbox": [85, 55, 362, 81], "category": "header", "text": "TPS562200, TPS563200\nSLVSCB0E – JANUARY 2014 – REVISED MAY 2023"}, {"bbox": [751, 34, 913, 80], "category": "header", "text": "TEXAS INSTRUMENTS\nwww.ti.com"}, {"bbox": [88, 93, 419, 110], "category": "title", "text": "6.7 Typical Characteristics TPS562200"}, {"bbox": [89, 117, 329, 132], "category": "text", "text": "$V_{IN}$ = 12 V (unless otherwise noted)."}, {"bbox": [125, 142, 479, 334], "category": "figure", "text": ""}, {"bbox": [132, 336, 458, 350], "category": "figure_caption", "text": "Figure 6-1. Supply Current vs Junction Temperature"}, {"bbox": [545, 142, 887, 336], "category": "figure", "text": ""}, {"bbox": [516, 356, 888, 370], "category": "figure_caption", "text": "Figure 6-2. VIN Shutdown Current vs Junction Temperature"}, {"bbox": [116, 375, 479, 584], "category": "figure", "text": ""}, {"bbox": [144, 589, 446, 603], "category": "figure_caption", "text": "Figure 6-3. En Current vs En Voltage"}, {"bbox": [111, 610, 462, 800], "category": "figure", "text": ""}, {"bbox": [169, 802, 422, 816], "category": "figure_caption", "text": "Figure 6-5. Efficiency vs Output Current"}, {"bbox": [86, 951, 96, 962], "category": "footer", "text": "8"}, {"bbox": [128, 950, 296, 963], "category": "footer", "text": "Submit Document Feedback"}, {"bbox": [657, 950, 912, 962], "category": "footer", "text": "Copyright © 2023 Texas Instruments Incorporated"}, {"bbox": [363, 975, 636, 987], "category": "footer", "text": "Product Folder Links: TPS562200 TPS563200"}]
```
